0: Fig.1: SICA's self-improvement, learning and adapting based on its past versions
1: SICA underwent significant self-improvement, leading to advancements in code
2: editing and navigation. Initially, SICA utilized a basic file-overwriting approach for
3: code changes. It subsequently developed a "Smart Editor" capable of more intelligent
4: and contextual edits. This evolved into a "Diff-Enhanced Smart Editor," incorporating
5: diffs for targeted modifications and pattern-based editing, and a "Quick Overwrite
7: SICA further implemented "Minimal Diff Output Optimization" and "Context-Sensitive
8: Diff Minimization," using Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) parsing for efficiency. Additionally,
9: a "SmartEditor Input Normalizer" was added. In terms of navigation, SICA
10: independently created an "AST Symbol Locator," using the code's structural map
11: (AST) to identify definitions within the codebase. Later, a "Hybrid Symbol Locator"
12: was developed, combining a quick search with AST checking. This was further
13: optimized via "Optimized AST Parsing in Hybrid Symbol Locator" to focus on relevant
14: code sections, improving search speed.(see Fig. 2)
